title: China-to-South Korea Solar Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: China Baseline Documentation vs Korean Market Entry Package
rows.target_requirement: A Korean market-entry package for a grid-tied PV inverter normally combines KC safety certification, applicable KS C/IEC test evidence, KN EMC or radio conformity where applicable, Korean labels and manuals, and KEPCO project interconnection acceptance. These requirements are cumulative: passing one gate does not waive the others.
rows.china_equivalent: A typical China-origin file set may include GB/T 37408-2019, GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004-2018, IEC CB reports, CQC/CGC certificates, Chinese EMC reports, and Chinese labels/manuals. This is a useful starting point but not a Korean approval package.
rows.gap: The export gap is procedural and documentary as much as technical. Build a clause-by-clause Korea crosswalk, confirm KC scope before import, retest or review against KS C/KN criteria, localize labels and manuals, and plan KEPCO interconnection evidence for the actual project configuration.

mandatory_status.eu: Mixed but cumulative — KC safety is compulsory where in scope; KN EMC/radio is mandatory where in scope; KEPCO approval is mandatory for grid connection.
mandatory_status.cn: Mixed — many China baseline inverter documents are voluntary or project-driven and do not transfer automatically.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not treat the China file set as a Korean approval file. Korea needs a KC/KS C/KN/KEPCO-specific compliance package.

source.publisher: Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy (MOTIE), Republic of Korea

rows.requirement: EMC and Radio Compliance — KN Standards and Korean EMC Registration
rows.target_requirement: South Korea regulates electromagnetic compatibility through Korean KN standards and conformity assessment administered by the National Radio Research Agency (RRA) under the radio-wave framework. Grid-tied PV inverters can be subject to EMC limits for conducted and radiated emissions and immunity; models with Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, LTE, or other radio modules may also require radio equipment conformity assessment. EMC/radio compliance is separate from KC safety certification.
target_standards: Radio Waves Act conformity assessment framework
RRA conformity assessment for broadcasting and communications equipment
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese inverter EMC evidence may include GB/T 17799 series, GB/T 17626 series, GB/T 37408-2019 EMC clauses, or SRRC approval for radio modules. These reports are useful technical references but do not automatically satisfy Korean KN standards, Korean test methods, labeling, or RRA registration requirements.
china_standards: SRRC radio type approval where radio modules are present
rows.gap: The main gap is Korean EMC administrative conformity, not only lab performance. Exporters should confirm the exact KN standard set, test the final SKU including communication modules and cables, and complete Korean registration/labeling before placing the product on the market.

mandatory_status.eu: Mandatory where the inverter or its communication module is within Korean EMC/radio conformity-assessment scope.
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or voluntary depending on Chinese EMC/radio product scope; not transferable to Korean RRA conformity.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat KN EMC/RRA review as a separate Korean gate, especially for smart inverters with communication modules.

source.publisher: National Radio Research Agency (RRA), Republic of Korea

rows.requirement: KC Safety Certification — Electrical Appliances and Consumer Products Safety Control Act
rows.target_requirement: For in-scope electrical appliances, South Korea requires KC safety certification under the Electrical Appliances and Consumer Products Safety Control Act (전기용품 및 생활용품 안전관리법). Grid-tied PV inverters that fall within the regulated electrical-appliance scope must be tested by a designated Korean body, certified before sale or import, and marked with the KC mark. This is compulsory where in scope, not a voluntary quality mark. KATS is the competent standards and product-safety authority under MOTIE.
target_standards: Electrical Appliances and Consumer Products Safety Control Act (전기용품 및 생활용품 안전관리법)
KC mark / Korea Certification safety scheme
KATS product safety implementation notices and designated certification bodies
rows.china_equivalent: China's common baseline for grid-tied PV inverters is GB/T 37408-2019 and voluntary CQC/CGC certification. Grid-tied PV inverters are generally treated outside the CCC mandatory catalogue, so Chinese documentation often does not include a compulsory product-safety certificate equivalent to Korea's KC safety certificate.
rows.gap: A Chinese voluntary CQC/CGC certificate or GB/T test report does not replace KC safety certification. The importer or manufacturer must confirm Korean scope classification, apply through a designated Korean certification body where required, complete Korean test/report review, and use the KC mark only after certification is granted.

mandatory_status.eu: Mandatory where in scope — KC safety certification and KC marking are compulsory for regulated electrical appliances.
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary for the common grid-tied PV inverter route where CCC is not applicable.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat KC safety as a compulsory Korean market-access gate when the inverter is in scope. Do not present Chinese voluntary certification as a KC substitute.

source.publisher: Korean Agency for Technology and Standards (KATS)

rows.requirement: KEPCO Distributed-Generation Interconnection — Distribution Grid Technical Criteria
rows.target_requirement: Connecting a grid-tied PV inverter system in South Korea requires project-level acceptance by Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) under the distributed-generation distribution-system interconnection technical criteria (분산형전원 배전계통 연계 기술기준) and KEPCO interconnection procedures. The review covers protection coordination, voltage and frequency operating settings, anti-islanding, power quality, reverse power, and system-impact limits. Product KC certification does not itself grant grid-connection approval.
target_standards: KEPCO distributed-generation distribution-system interconnection technical criteria (분산형전원 배전계통 연계 기술기준)
KEPCO interconnection application and technical review procedures
rows.china_equivalent: China uses GB/T 19964-2024, GB/T 37408-2019, NB/T 32004-2018, and utility connection agreements with State Grid or China Southern Power Grid. These documents show Chinese grid-code alignment but do not bind KEPCO or replace Korean project review.
rows.gap: Korean interconnection is a separate utility gate. A China grid-code type test must be rechecked against KEPCO settings and the project point of common coupling. Firmware protection settings, Korean frequency/voltage windows, islanding behavior, and power-quality evidence should be prepared for KEPCO review.

mandatory_status.eu: Mandatory for grid connection — KEPCO acceptance is required before the system is connected to the distribution network.
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory in practice through Chinese utility interconnection agreements, but not transferable to KEPCO approval.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] KC product certification and Chinese grid-code evidence are not enough for energization in Korea; KEPCO interconnection acceptance must be handled separately.

rows.requirement: KS C / IEC Product Standards for PV Inverter Safety and Performance
rows.target_requirement: Korean KC safety assessment commonly relies on Korean Standards in the KS C electrical series, including Korean adoptions of IEC inverter safety and PV power-converter standards such as IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2, plus inverter performance and grid-support requirements referenced by Korean certification bodies. The exact KS C edition and test scope must be confirmed against the KC application category and the certification body's current rules.
target_standards: KATS Korean Standards (KS) catalogue and KC certification criteria
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese manufacturers may hold IEC 62109 CB Scheme reports, GB/T 37408-2019 test reports, or voluntary CQC/CGC certificates. These can support technical review but do not automatically establish conformity to the Korean KS C edition, national deviations, Korean markings, or KC administrative requirements.
rows.gap: The practical gap is edition mapping and national-deviation review. Exporters should prepare a standards crosswalk from IEC/GB clauses to the applicable KS C clauses and identify any Korean deviations, labeling instructions, and factory-inspection requirements imposed by the KC certification body.

mandatory_status.eu: Mandatory where incorporated into KC certification criteria for the regulated product scope.
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary unless required by project owner, buyer, or grid-connection contract.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC or GB evidence should be treated as supporting evidence only until mapped to the applicable KS C/KC certification criteria.

source.publisher: Korean Agency for Technology and Standards (KATS)

rows.requirement: Korean Marking, Importer Responsibility, and Market Surveillance
rows.target_requirement: KC-regulated products must carry the required Korean certification information, KC mark, model identification, electrical ratings, manufacturer or importer information, warnings, and user instructions required by the applicable KC safety and EMC rules. The Korean importer is a key responsible party for placing imported electrical appliances on the Korean market and for maintaining certification, labeling, and recall/market-surveillance records.
target_standards: Electrical Appliances and Consumer Products Safety Control Act
RRA/KC EMC labeling rules where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese domestic labels and manuals usually reference GB standards, Chinese importer or manufacturer data, and Chinese warning text. They do not satisfy Korean-language labeling or Korean importer-responsibility requirements unless localized and tied to the Korean certification records.
china_standards: Chinese product label and manual requirements
rows.gap: Export files should include Korean labels, Korean manuals, KC certificate numbers, model-family mapping, importer identity, and rating-plate artwork. A technically compliant inverter can still be blocked or recalled if certification numbers, model names, or Korean warnings do not match the approved records.

mandatory_status.eu: Mandatory where KC safety or EMC conformity applies.
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for China domestic placement but not accepted as Korean labeling.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Korean labeling and importer-record alignment should be checked before shipment, not after customs or KEPCO review.

source.publisher: Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy (MOTIE), Republic of Korea
